title: leucanthemum atratum subsp. coronopifolium
intro: Leucanthemum atratum subsp. coronopifolium, also known as the coronopifolium daisy or pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of insects, bacteria, and fungi. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Leucanthemum atratum subsp. coronopifolium can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural remedy enthusiasts.
